body: $NVDA

Just saw $DELL deliver the first NVDA Vera Rubin NVL72 rack into $CRWV. This is the first real deployment of $NVDA next-gen rack architecture hitting production environments, not just roadmap talk anymore. 
 
As a trader watching AI infra names, this is how early-cycle expansion starts. $NVDA keeps pushing system-level dominance, while DELL quietly becomes the execution backbone for full rack delivery. CRWV is now sitting right in the first wave of next-gen AI data center buildout. 
 
Tape is still mixed, but these infra milestones usually lead sentiment in semis before numbers show up. Watching for follow-through across $NVDA supply chain into next sessions.

body:
First wave of rack deployments rolling out through 2030 — the value chain splits across clear winners: 
 
GPUs dominate at 50%, led by $NVDA. 
 
Memory takes 25%, driven by HBM4 and LPDDR5X, with $MU, Samsung, and SK hynix positioned in the flow. 
 
Networking & interconnect accounts for 10%, where $ANET, MRVL, AVGO, and CIEN sit. 
 
Power and liquid cooling make up 7%, led by $VRT, ETN, PWR, and MOD. 
 
Server integration and rack assembly represent 5%, including $DELL, SMCI, and HPQ. 
 
The remaining 3% is advanced packaging & optics, featuring TSM, AMKR, LITE, COHR, and AAOI.

message.body:
Something very rare has taken place in the markets! 
 
Over the trailing 2-month period, S&P 500 has only traded below the 10-DMA on a single trading day. The last time this occurred was in late 2023 (top chart). The relentless rally in the market continued through the 1st half of 2024.  
 
Before that, 2 occurrences in 2010. Of the 3 prior occurrences, markets were higher 6, 9, and 12 months forward.  
 
$SPY $QQQ $NVDA $AAPL $UVXY

body:
$BB $NVDA If robotics truly is the future and BlackBerry maintains market share of the robotic operating system market, this stock will 50x!  
 
Its one of those plays you kind of just need to set it and forget it. This is a real long term play even though there has been a lot of short term hype. 
 
But the hype is very real and who knows if price will ever come back down to the single digits. 
 
This is a buy the ticket and take the ride kind of play. 
 
Not telling you to go all in but just buy the ticket because it can be exceptionally valuable one day. 
 
Wall Street still pricing BlackBerry like it still makes phones. That’s how you know you’re still early...

message.body:
$BB $NVDA All robotics need a software that makes their tasks 100% precise and 100% safety certified, required by every single government 
 
That’s where BlackBerry comes in with their QNX software. 
 
That’s why NVDIA has partnered and implemented QNX into their software stack  
 
This isn’t a decision for robotic company’s, they NEED to implement safety certified operating systems for their robotics 
 
And what still blows my mind is the operating system for robotics, BB's QNX, is only trading at a $5.3B marketcap. 
 
The risk reward here is incredible !

$NVDA $TSLA 
 
The Physical AI era is emerging: a ~$40T robotics revolution 
 
AI is shifting from software into embodied hardware intelligence. NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang sees humanoid robots unlocking a ~$40 trillion labor automation market, driving strong capital positioning. 
 
Physical AI is essentially digital intelligence with a body. Autonomous driving and humanoid robots share the same core stack of perception, planning, and control, with scale potential beyond smartphones and EVs. 
 
The market is forming around two pillars: NVIDIA as the infrastructure layer powering training and compute via chips and the Isaac platform; and Tesla as one of the few players pushing humanoid robots toward real commercialization. Capital inflows continue to signal an early but accelerating industrial cycle.

The market is forming around two pillars: NVIDIA as the infrastructure layer powering training and compute via chips and the Isaac platform; and Tesla as one of the few players pushing humanoid robots toward real commercialization. If Trump ran again...

Taiwan is back at the center of the AI chip cycle.  
 
Computex 2026 opens June 2 in Taipei and the entire semiconductor leadership is converging around AI infrastructure and supply chain expansion. 
 
 $NVDA $QCOM $MRVL $NXPI $INT.X are not just attending, they are effectively setting the tone for the next 3 to 5 years of compute demand and capacity planning.  
 
With Jensen Huang, Lisa Su, and Lip Bu Tan all present, the message is clear: the AI hardware race is tightening at the highest level.  
 
From AI servers to robotics, this event is where roadmaps get positioned before the market fully reacts.  
 
Expect sentiment and flow shifts across semis into and after the summit.

body:
$NVDA May 30 (Reuters) - AI chip firm Nvidia(NVDA) and Microsoft(MSFT) are expected next week to debut the first Windows PCs that use Nvidia's(NVDA) chips as the main processor, Axios reported on Saturday, citing sources. 
 
Nvidia (NVDA)-powered computers are expected both from Microsoft's(MSFT) Surface brand as well as other computer makers including Dell. 
 
Microsoft's (MSFT) efforts to shift to more battery-life-friendly chips have yet to drive a significant sales boom. Its primary rival Apple, which uses its own chips, unveiled updated MacBooks featuring its latest M5-series chips in March.  
 
Microsoft (MSFT) and Nvidia(NVDA) will unveil the new PCs at the Computex trade show in Taiwan and Microsoft's(MSFT) Build developer conference in San Francisco, the report said.

Nvidia (NVDA)-powered computers are expected both from Microsoft's(MSFT) Surface brand as well as other computer makers including Dell. 
 
Microsoft's (MSFT) efforts to shift to more battery-life-friendly chips have yet to drive a significant sales boom. Its primary rival Apple, which uses its own chips, unveiled updated MacBooks featuring its latest M5-series chips in March.  
 
Microsoft (MSFT) and Nvidia(NVDA) will unveil the new PCs at the Computex trade show in Taiwan and Microsoft's(MSFT) Build developer conference in San Francisco, the report said.

message.body:
$AMZN $META $MSFT $NVDA 

We are in a bubble. Forward P/E’s are based on pro forma data. Dangerous at that. 

Adjust these for macro headwinds alone and the valuations quickly become more expensive. 

Adjust based on a closer earnings timetable and the historical looks more appropriate. 

A small hiccup can cause any of these to nosedive.

body:
$NVDA $MU $SNDK $LITE 
 
EU AI infra is repricing. SoftBank rolling a €75B (~$82B) France AI buildout, with ~€45B (~$49B) first phase over 5 years targeting ~5GW data center capacity. This is real capex into compute, not just headline noise. 
 
Europe has been lagging US/China due to energy costs, but this scale of investment forces demand across memory, storage, optics and GPU ecosystems. 
 
If execution holds through 2031, it’s structurally bullish for AI hardware: NVDA compute demand, MU HBM bandwidth, SNDK storage expansion, LITE optical/interconnect. 
 
Still think market underestimates how much power per GW AI actually consumes. Follow infrastructure capex, not sentiment.

data.text:
EU AI infra is repricing. SoftBank rolling a €75B (~$82B) France AI buildout, with ~€45B (~$49B) first phase over 5 years targeting ~5GW data center capacity. This is real capex into compute, not just headline noise. 
 
Europe has been lagging US/China due to energy costs, but this scale of investment forces demand across memory, storage, optics and GPU ecosystems. 
 
If execution holds through 2031, it’s structurally bullish for AI hardware: NVDA compute demand, MU HBM bandwidth, SNDK storage expansion, LITE optical/interconnect. 
 
Still think market underestimates how much power per GW AI actually consumes. Follow infrastructure capex, not sentiment.

body:
$SPX implied move has slipped to ~102pts, even softer than last holiday week, but don’t mistake it for a quiet tape.  
 
This week is stacked: $NVDA conference, a wave of semis + software earnings, and the monthly Jobs Report.  
 
Catalysts are everywhere, yet price action keeps grinding into consolidation instead of a clean trend. 
 
Vol sellers look comfortable, and intraday chop is becoming the default environment.  
 
With positioning light and volatility compressed, liquidity pockets matter more than direction.  
 
Early-week drift may mask the real move into data releases.  
 
Risk here isn’t lack of news, it’s overcompressed volatility waiting for a break.  
 
Feels like the market is already leaning into next week’s SpaceX IPO narrative, and once $SPX wakes up, the expansion could be sharp.

Something very rare has taken place in the markets! 
 
Over the trailing 2-month period, S&P 500 has only traded below the 10-DMA on a single trading day. The last time this occurred was in late 2023 (top chart). The relentless rally in the market continued through the 1st half of 2024.  
 
Before that, 2 occurrences in 2010. Of the 3 prior occurrences, markets were higher 6, 9, and 12 months forward.
